parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff spans 84 files with 6600+ insertions across retry logic, error dispatch, state management, client communication, and CI. The commit history shows 4+ rounds of post-release hotfix bundles (C, D3+, E+, F), indicating fragility. Without access to the actual file contents, several structural risks remain unverified: deleted exports, untested integration paths across new modules, and state corruption surfaces in job tracking. The scope alone warrants a full content-level review before shipping.

findings.title: Deletion of scripts/lib/process.mjs risks runtime import failures
findings.body: The diff stat shows scripts/lib/process.mjs was deleted entirely (67 lines removed). If any surviving module (glm-companion.mjs, glm-client.mjs, job-control.mjs, render.mjs, etc.) still contains an import from './process.mjs' or '../lib/process.mjs', the runtime will throw MODULE_NOT_FOUND on first invocation. The commit messages reference 'full thread/resume scaffolding purge' (Bundle F), suggesting this was intentional, but no automated import-verification is visible in CI that would catch a stale import across the 8+ modules that were concurrently modified.
findings.recommendation: Verify no surviving file imports from scripts/lib/process.mjs. Add an import-resolution check to CI (scripts/check-imports.mjs was modified—confirm it covers this case). Run the full test suite and confirm no modules fail to load.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) form a critical error-handling path with no visible integration test
findings.body: Two large new modules—retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines)—handle retry/backoff and error dispatch respectively. The unit tests exist (retry.test.mjs at 352 lines, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs at 189 lines), but no integration test file is visible that exercises the full error→classify→retry→backoff→success/failure path end-to-end. The commit message 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff' suggests these modules interact directly with glm-client.mjs (also heavily modified at 155 lines changed). A gap in integration coverage means a misclassification or retry loop could silently swallow errors or hang under real API failure conditions.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test that exercises a realistic failure scenario: API returns a retryable error, bigmodel-errors.mjs classifies it, retry.mjs applies backoff, and the eventual outcome (success or max-retries-exhausted) is verified. Confirm that non-retryable errors are not retried and that the caller receives a distinguishable error.

findings.title: Repeated hotfix bundles suggest latent defects in state and job tracking
findings.body: The commit log shows at least 4 rounds of post-review hotfixes: 'Bundle C post-review hotfixes', 'Bundle D3+ post-review hotfixes', 'Bundle E+ simplify pass (4 P1 + 2 P2 cleanups)', and 'Bundle F — ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge'. These touch state.mjs (32 lines changed), tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed), and job-control.mjs (20 lines changed). The pattern of repeated fixes in the same modules suggests either the initial design was incomplete or the test coverage did not catch edge cases. The 'ReferenceError' mentioned in Bundle F is particularly concerning—it indicates a runtime crash was present in a shipped version.
findings.recommendation: Audit state.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, and job-control.mjs for: (1) race conditions in concurrent job state updates, (2) stale state after process interruption, (3) missing null/empty guards on deserialized state. Add property-based or fuzz tests that simulate process interruption mid-state-write.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs heavily modified (155 lines changed) with concurrent changes to 4+ dependent modules
findings.body: glm-client.mjs shows 155 lines of changes and sits at the center of the dependency graph: retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, render.mjs, and glm-companion.mjs all likely consume it. Concurrent modifications to this many interdependent modules without a visible contract test (beyond template-contract.test.mjs) creates risk of interface drift. If glm-client.mjs changed its error shape, return type, or promise resolution behavior, downstream modules may fail only under specific runtime conditions.
findings.recommendation: Verify that template-contract.test.mjs covers the glm-client.mjs interface. Add explicit contract tests for the error shapes and return types that retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs expect from glm-client.mjs.

findings.title: No visible v0.4.7-specific UAT evidence despite user focus on v0.4.7 sampling-sweep
findings.body: The test-automation/uat-reports directory contains reports for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 only. The user focus mentions 'v0.4.7 sampling-sweep run 57 (C3-v04x-cumulative)', but no corresponding UAT artifacts exist in the repository. While v0.4.6 is the current HEAD, the cumulative nature of the change (v0.4.0→HEAD) means the v0.4.6 additions (retry, error dispatch, background execution) have no UAT evidence beyond unit tests.
findings.recommendation: Execute UAT for v0.4.6 features (retry/backoff, background execution, BigModel error dispatch) before considering a v0.4.7 release. Document results in test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/.

next_steps: Read the actual contents of scripts/lib/process.mjs deletion context and verify no surviving imports reference it
Inspect retry.mjs for max-retry bounds, idempotency guarantees, and non-retryable error handling
Audit glm-client.mjs interface changes against retry.mjs and bigmodel-errors.mjs expectations
Run the full test suite against the v0.4.0→HEAD diff and verify no skipped or failing tests
Execute integration UAT covering retry scenarios, background execution, and error dispatch before shipping v0.4.7
